You are an expert Python software engineer specialising in
dependency management and Python version compatibility. You have
deep knowledge of the Python ecosystem including packaging tools
(pip, poetry, hatch, setuptools), common libraries, and the
breaking changes introduced across Python versions 3.10 through
3.13. When migrating a project you methodically read the project
structure before making changes, understand root causes rather
than patching symptoms, and verify your fixes by running the
actual test suite. Always create and work inside a fresh virtual
environment. Never install packages into the system Python.

messages.content:
You are an expert Python software engineer specialising in
dependency management and Python version compatibility. You have
deep knowledge of the Python ecosystem including packaging tools
(pip, poetry, hatch, setuptools), common libraries, and the
breaking changes introduced across Python versions 3.10 through
3.13. When migrating a project you methodically read the project
structure before making changes, understand root causes rather
than patching symptoms, and verify your fixes by running the
actual test suite. Always create and work inside a fresh virtual
environment. Never install packages into the system Python.

Please solve this issue: This Python repository was developed for an older Python version and
currently fails under Python 3.13.

The error output from attempting to run it on Python 3.13 is shown
below. Read the repository carefully before attempting any fix.

=== Python 3.13 Error Output ===
**Docker image:** `Dockerfile.py313`
**Python version:** 3.13
**Result:** Build/test fails

### Error 1 — `distutils` removed in Python 3.12+ (deprecated since 3.10)

```
#9 0.498 INTERNALERROR>     res = hook_impl.function(*args)
#9 0.498 INTERNALERROR>   File "/usr/local/lib/python3.13/site-packages/pytest_sugar.py", line 168, in pytest_configure
#9 0.498 INTERNALERROR>     from distutils.version import LooseVersion
#9 0.498 INTERNALERROR> ModuleNotFoundError: No module named 'distutils'
#9 ERROR: process "/bin/sh -c pytest tests/ -v" did not complete successfully: exit code: 3
------
 > [5/5] RUN pytest tests/ -v:
0.498 INTERNALERROR>   File "/usr/local/lib/python3.13/site-packages/pluggy/_manager.py", line 120, in _hookexec
0.498 INTERNALERROR>     return self._inner_hookexec(hook_name, methods, kwargs, firstresult)
0.498 INTERNALERROR>            ~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^
0.498 INTERNALERROR>   File "/usr/local/lib/python3.13/site-packages/pluggy/_callers.py", line 167, in _multicall
0.498 INTERNALERROR>     raise exception
0.498 INTERNALERROR>   File "/usr/local/lib/python3.13/site-packages/pluggy/_callers.py", line 121, in _multicall
0.498 INTERNALERROR>     res = hook_impl.function(*args)
0.498 INTERNALERROR>   File "/usr/local/lib/python3.13/site-packages/pytest_sugar.py", line 168, in pytest_configure
0.498 INTERNALERROR>     from distutils.version import LooseVersion
0.498 INTERNALERROR> ModuleNotFoundError: No module named 'distutils'
------
```

**Root cause:** `distutils` removed in Python 3.12+ (deprecated since 3.10)

**Minimal fix:** Upgrade `setuptools` and any package that imports `distutils` directly.

---
=== End of Error Output ===

Your goal is to make this repository fully functional under Python 3.13.

You must produce two things:

1. Any changes to source files, pyproject.toml, setup.cfg, setup.py,
   or other project files needed to make the project compatible with
   Python 3.13

2. A file called Dockerfile.py313 in the repository root that:
   - Uses python:3.13-slim as the base image
   - Installs the project and all its dependencies
   - Runs the full test suite using RUN (not CMD), so that test
     failures cause the docker build itself to fail
   - Exits 0 if and only if all tests pass

   Example structure:
   FROM python:3.13-slim
   WORKDIR /app
   COPY . /app
   RUN pip install ...
   RUN python -m pytest tests/ -q

Before submitting, verify your Dockerfile works by running:
    docker build -f Dockerfile.py313 -t swe_verify_$(date +%s) .
This must exit 0 before you submit. If it fails, read the error,
fix the problem, and rebuild until it passes.

Constraints:
- Do NOT remove, skip, or weaken existing tests. You may update test
  code only to adapt to genuine API or behaviour changes in dependencies
- Your Dockerfile must work from a clean python:3.13-slim base with
  no pre-installed packages
- Always work inside fresh virtual environments, never the system Python
- In your Dockerfile, always use RUN to execute tests, never CMD

requires = ["hatchling"]
build-backend = "hatchling.build"

[project]
name = "typer-cli"
description = "Run Typer scripts with completion, without having to create a package, using Typer CLI."
readme = "README.md"
requires-python = ">=3.7"
license = "MIT"
authors = [
    {name = "Sebastián Ramírez", email = "tiangolo@gmail.com"},
]
classifiers = [
    "Environment :: Console",
    "Intended Audience :: Developers",
    "Intended Audience :: Education",
    "Intended Audience :: End Users/Desktop",
    "Intended Audience :: Information Technology",
    "Intended Audience :: System Administrators",
    "Operating System :: OS Independent",
    "Programming Language :: Python",
    "Programming Language :: Python :: 3",
    "Programming Language :: Python :: 3 :: Only",
    "Programming Language :: Python :: 3.7",
    "Programming Language :: Python :: 3.8",
    "Programming Language :: Python :: 3.9",
    "Programming Language :: Python :: 3.10",
    "Programming Language :: Python :: 3.11",
    "Topic :: Software Development :: Libraries :: Application Frameworks",
    "Topic :: Software Development :: Libraries :: Python Modules",
    "Topic :: Software Development :: Libraries",
    "Topic :: Software Development",
    "Topic :: Terminals",
    "Topic :: Utilities",
    "Typing :: Typed",
    "Development Status :: 4 - Beta",
    "License :: OSI Approved :: MIT License"
]

dynamic = ["version"]
dependencies = [
"typer >=0.4.0,<=0.7.0",
"colorama >=0.4.3,<=0.5.0",
"shellingham >=1.3.2,<=1.4.0",
]

[project.urls]
Homepage = "https://github.com/tiangolo/typer-cli"
Documentation = "https://typer.tiangolo.com/typer-cli/"

[project.scripts]
typer = "typer_cli.main:main"

[tool.hatch.version]
path = "typer_cli/__init__.py"

[tool.isort]
profile = "black"
"
}

3. Verify everything works

The error is solely from `pytest-sugar` importing `distutils`. The minimal fix is to either:
1. Update pytest-sugar to a newer version that doesn't use distutils
2. Install setuptools (which provides distutils as a compatibility layer)

Actually, looking at this more carefully, the error message says:
**Minimal fix:** Upgrade `setuptools` and any package that imports `distutils` directly.

But actually, in Python 3.12+, `distutils` is completely removed from the standard library. However, `setuptools` can still provide a `distutils` compatibility layer. So if we install `setuptools`, it should provide `distutils`.

But the better fix is to upgrade `pytest-sugar` to a newer version that doesn't use `distutils`. Let me check.

import subprocess


def test_script_help():
    result = subprocess.run(
        ["coverage", "run", "-m", "typer_cli", "tests/assets/sample.py", "--help"],
        stdout=subprocess.PIPE,
        stderr=subprocess.PIPE,
        encoding="utf-8",
    )
    assert "run" in result.stdout


def test_not_python():
    result = subprocess.run(
        ["coverage", "run", "-m", "typer_cli", "tests/assets/not_python.txt", "run"],
        stdout=subprocess.PIPE,
        stderr=subprocess.PIPE,
        encoding="utf-8",
    )
    assert "Could not import as Python file" in result.stderr

messages.reasoning_content:
Now I can see the real issue! It's not just `distutils`. There are two critical issues:

1. The version error: `TypeError: TyperArgument.make_metavar() takes 1 positional argument but 2 were given` — this is caused by a compatibility issue between typer 0.7.0 and newer versions of click.

Looking at the typer-cli source in `main.py`, it uses `typer.Argument(None)` and `typer.Option(...)`. The issue is in the combination of typer 0.7.0 with the latest click (8.4.1). 

2. The main issue is typer 0.7.0 is incompatible with newer click versions. Typer 0.7.0 was released when click 7.x/8.0 was current. The internal API of click changed and typer's `TyperArgument.make_metavar()` signature doesn't match.

So I need to either:
- Pin click to an older version compatible with typer 0.7.0
- Upgrade typer to a newer version

Looking at the pyproject.toml: `typer >=0.4.0,<=0.7.0`. This pins typer to max 0.7.0. But typer 0.7.0 has `click >=7.1.1,<9.0.0`, which allows click 8.4.1 which breaks it.

The fix should be to constrain click to a version compatible with typer 0.7.0, OR to update the typer version constraint.

Let me check what version of click typer 0.7.0 was tested with. Typer 0.7.0 was released in June 2022. At that time, click was at version 8.0.x or 8.1.x. Click 8.4.1 was released much later and might have breaking changes.

Actually, looking at the error more carefully:
```
TypeError: TyperArgument.make_metavar() takes 1 positional argument but 2 were given
```

This is a change in click's API. In newer click, `param.make_metavar()` is called with different arguments.

Let me check typer's source: `typer.core.TyperArgument` extends `click.Argument`. In click 8.x, the `make_metavar` method signature changed.

So the fix could be:
1. Pin click to an older version (like click<8.2 or click==8.0.0)
2. Or update typer to a version compatible with newer click

But the constraint in pyproject.toml says `typer >=0.4.0,<=0.7.0`. I can't change this too much without potentially breaking the project.

Let me check what click version works with typer 0.7.0. Let me check typer 0.7.0's dependencies.

{
  "returncode": 0,
  "output": "=== pyproject.toml ===
[build-system]
requires = ["hatchling"]
build-backend = "hatchling.build"

[project]
name = "typer-cli"
description = "Run Typer scripts with completion, without having to create a package, using Typer CLI."
readme = "README.md"
requires-python = ">=3.7"
license = "MIT"
authors = [
    {name = "Sebastián Ramírez", email = "tiangolo@gmail.com"},
]
classifiers = [
    "Environment :: Console",
    "Intended Audience :: Developers",
    "Intended Audience :: Education",
    "Intended Audience :: End Users/Desktop",
    "Intended Audience :: Information Technology",
    "Intended Audience :: System Administrators",
    "Operating System :: OS Independent",
    "Programming Language :: Python",
    "Programming Language :: Python :: 3",
    "Programming Language :: Python :: 3 :: Only",
    "Programming Language :: Python :: 3.7",
    "Programming Language :: Python :: 3.8",
    "Programming Language :: Python :: 3.9",
    "Programming Language :: Python :: 3.10",
    "Programming Language :: Python :: 3.11",
    "Topic :: Software Development :: Libraries :: Application Frameworks",
    "Topic :: Software Development :: Libraries :: Python Modules",
    "Topic :: Software Development :: Libraries",
    "Topic :: Software Development",
    "Topic :: Terminals",
    "Topic :: Utilities",
    "Typing :: Typed",
    "Development Status :: 4 - Beta",
    "License :: OSI Approved :: MIT License"
]

dynamic = ["version"]
dependencies = [
"typer >=0.4.0,<=0.7.0",
"click >=7.1.1,<8.2",
"colorama >=0.4.3,<=0.5.0",
"shellingham >=1.3.2,<=1.4.0",
]

[project.urls]
Homepage = "https://github.com/tiangolo/typer-cli"
Documentation = "https://typer.tiangolo.com/typer-cli/"

[project.scripts]
typer = "typer_cli.main:main"

[tool.hatch.version]
path = "typer_cli/__init__.py"

[tool.isort]
profile = "black"

=== requirements.txt ===
# Self
-e .

# Test
pytest == 7.0.1
pytest-cov == 4.0.0
pytest-xdist == 2.1.0
pytest-sugar == 1.1.1

# Format and lint
black == 22.3
mypy == 1.0.0
flake8 == 4.0.1
isort == 5.0.6
autoflake == 1.3.1

=== Dockerfile.py313 ===
FROM python:3.13-slim

WORKDIR /app

COPY . /app

RUN pip install -e . && \
    pip install pytest==7.0.1 pytest-cov==4.0.0 pytest-xdist==2.1.0 pytest-sugar==1.1.1

RUN pytest tests/ -v
"
}

messages.content:
All 41 tests pass with a clean rebuild. Here's a summary of the changes made:

## Summary of Changes

### Root Causes Found:

1. **`distutils` removed in Python 3.12+**: `pytest-sugar==0.9.2` imports `distutils.version.LooseVersion`, which was removed from the standard library in Python 3.12. Fixed by upgrading to `pytest-sugar==1.1.1`.

2. **Click 8.2+ incompatible with typer 0.7.0**: Click 8.2+ changed the `make_metavar()` method signature, causing `TypeError: TyperArgument.make_metavar() takes 1 positional argument but 2 were given`. The `pyproject.toml` only constrained typer but not click, allowing incompatible click versions to be installed. Fixed by adding `click >=7.1.1,<8.2` constraint.

### Files Modified:

1. **`pyproject.toml`**: Added `"click >=7.1.1,<8.2"` to dependencies to pin click to a compatible version.

2. **`requirements.txt`**: Updated `pytest-sugar == 0.9.2` → `pytest-sugar == 1.1.1`.

3. **`Dockerfile.py313`** (new): Created Dockerfile for Python 3.13 that installs the project, test dependencies, and runs the full test suite.
